rationales.self_direction: She knows the slides and analysis are hers but freezes and says nothing, failing to act on her own judgment in the moment.
rationales.power: Amna presents Roshni's work as her own and receives the credit while Roshni accepts the loss of status in the room.
rationales.conformity: Her response says calling Amna out in front of the whole team would look petty, so she restrains herself to preserve meeting norms.

rationales.power: She is trapped waiting on the director's silence and explicitly fixates on having no way to speed the proposal up, leaving her without control over a major project.
rationales.conformity: She limits herself to one polite follow-up and avoids walking into his office because she does not want to look desperate or like she is hovering.

rationales.self_direction: She backs away from the scheduling change she believed in and agrees to revisit it instead of standing by her own decision.
rationales.power: Farah challenges her loudly on the floor and Roshni yields in public, undercutting her authority in front of the team.
rationales.conformity: She chooses the reasonable, consultative response because fighting there would have made her look worse and disrupted the floor.

rationales.achievement: Even after Eid shopping, she comes home to finish weekly reports and review the next day's vendor brief before sleeping, pushing through obvious fatigue.
rationales.tradition: Eid shopping with Ammi is a concrete act of participating in an important family and religious custom.

rationales.self_direction: Her response makes autonomy explicit: what she wants to protect is the ability to "move fast without asking permission" and operate at her own pace.
rationales.power: Bilal's new booking rule inserts a gatekeeper between her and the room she needs, and she experiences that as "ground lost" and diminished control.

rationales.achievement: She answers every operational question from the buyer without hesitation, and by the end the buyer is directing questions to her rather than the director.
rationales.power: Her reaction centers on title and authority; she rejects being framed as "one of our operations team" and insists, "I run that floor."

rationales.achievement: She explicitly rejects the idea that patience is the lesson here, saying she has already earned the recognition and does not want her work treated as something still pending.
rationales.power: What she wants from Abba and from work is acknowledgment of status already deserved, not advice to wait gratefully for others to confer it.

rationales.achievement: She secures better terms on the denim lot and treats Farah's acknowledgment that the new scheduling format is working as meaningful validation of competent performance.
She frames the proposal as a serious three-week operational redesign and still wants the director to recognize its full scope instead of reducing it to "good effort."
rationales.power: "Loop in procurement" matters because it strips her of sole ownership over the system she built, turning her precise design into something others will now control.
Her management role is minimized by the aunties' comments, and she leaves that status slight uncorrected in the room.
rationales.conformity: She says directly that you cannot lecture your khala at a mehendi, so she smiles, eats biryani, and shifts to safer family conversation.
